*class definition*
The C++ class named UndergraduateStudent encapsulates the core data for a university learner including their full legal name and unique identifier code alongside the cumulative grade point average metric.

*member variables*
Private attributes store the complete legal name as a string, the distinctive student ID as an integer, and the floating-point value representing the academic grade point average.

*constructor logic*
The initialization function accepts arguments for the name, ID, and GPA to ensure every new object is fully configured with valid data upon creation.

*accessor methods*
Public getter functions provide read-only access to the stored name, the unique identifier code, and the current grade point average without allowing direct modification.
